---
title: ""
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

::::: hero-section
:::: hero-content
Wildlife Disease Ecology · One Health · Conservation Physiology

# Alan J. Figueroa Ruiz

## Conservation agronomist and M.S. Biology student studying pathogen movement across human-modified landscapes.

My work explores how environmental change, human activity, and pathogen exposure affect wildlife health, with a focus on *Toxoplasma gondii* transmission across terrestrial, estuarine, and marine systems in Puerto Rico.

::: hero-buttons
[Explore my research](research.qmd){.button-primary}\

[View projects](projects.qmd){.button-secondary}\

[View CV](cv.qmd){.button-secondary}
:::
::::
:::::

::: section-block
## Current focus

I am a master's student in Biology at the University of Puerto Rico at Mayagüez, working at the intersection of wildlife disease ecology, conservation physiology, One Health, environmental health, and reproducible research.

My current research focuses on the land-sea transmission of *Toxoplasma gondii* in Puerto Rico and its potential conservation implications for the endangered Caribbean manatee. I am especially interested in how human-modified landscapes, stormwater runoff, feral and community cats, coastal ecosystems, and pathogen exposure connect terrestrial and marine health.
:::

:::::::: section-block
## Research themes

::::::: card-grid
::: info-card
### Wildlife disease ecology

Understanding how pathogens move through ecosystems and affect threatened wildlife populations.
:::

::: info-card
### One Health

Connecting human, animal, and environmental health through island-based conservation science.
:::

::: info-card
### Conservation physiology

Exploring how stressors, disease, and environmental change shape organismal health and survival.
:::

::: info-card
### Reproducible research

Using R, GitHub, bioinformatics, mapping, and open workflows to make research transparent and reusable.
:::
:::::::
::::::::

:::::::: section-block
## Selected projects

::::::: project-grid
::: project-card
### Manatee Project Puerto Rico

Environmental transmission of *Toxoplasma gondii* across terrestrial, estuarine, and marine systems in Puerto Rico.

[Read more →](projects.qmd)
:::

::: project-card
### Human *T. gondii* epidemiology

Retrospective analysis of human diagnostic records to characterize evidence of exposure in Puerto Rico.

[Read more →](projects.qmd)
:::

::: project-card
### Zebrafish ZF4 infection model

Cellular and tissue-based work using zebrafish ZF4 cells infected with *Toxoplasma gondii*.

[Read more →](projects.qmd)
:::

::: project-card
### WildCiencia

Science communication and community-based conservation outreach focused on island science.

[Read more →](outreach.qmd)
:::
:::::::
::::::::

:::: section-block
## Website sections

::: link-grid
[Publications & Presentations](publications.qmd)

[Teaching](teaching.qmd)

[Outreach](outreach.qmd)

[News](news.qmd)

[CV](cv.qmd)

[Contact](contact.qmd)
:::
::::
